top_headline: BEIJING DELIVERS — WARSH CONFIRMED 54-45 · PPI +6.0% SHOCK · SPX/NDX SIMULTANEOUS ATH
chapters: I · MACRO — PPI April +6.0% YoY vs +4.9% est — hottest since Dec 2022; MoM +1.4% vs +0.5%; gasoline +15.6% MoM; core +5.2% vs +4.3%; TNX spiked to 4.49%

I · MACRO — Warsh confirmed 54-45 May 13 — narrowest modern Fed vote; one Dem crossover (Fetterman); Powell exits May 16; first FOMC June 16-17; inherits CPI 3.8% + PPI 6.0% + Brent $108 + rate hike risk 30-35%

II · AI TRADE — NVDA T-6 to May 20: Huang boards Air Force One in Anchorage after Trump call; H200 chip framework discussed in Beijing (~$3.5-4B annual China revenue); May 13 close $225.83 +2.29%; Goldman EPS $1.86 above $1.77 consensus; BofA PT $320
III · EARNINGS — CSCO Q3 FY2026 record revenue $15.84B vs $15.54B est (+12% YoY); EPS $1.06 vs $1.04; surged ~17% AH on AI networking demand from hyperscalers
III · EARNINGS — BABA FY2026 Q4 RMB 243.38B (~$35.3B) missed est; swung to operating loss on AI/cloud capex; 1T yuan FY milestone; stock +6.5% on summit tailwinds + cloud optionality

IV · GEOPOLITICS — Trump-Xi Day 1 Beijing: Boeing 500-aircraft (86% Kalshi prob), soybeans 25M t/yr, H200 licensing framework, rare earths partial relief; Musk: 'Many good things achieved'; Xi: 'Partners not rivals' / Thucydides Trap
V · TRACK RECORD — No-005/No-007 review: NVDA long $217→$225.83 WIN +4.07%; BRENT short $101.96→$108 LOSS −5.92%; BRENT short $104.49→$108 LOSS −3.36%; 1W/2L/0F avg −1.73%

flagged_setups.thesis: Dual-catalyst pre-earnings setup with T-6 days to May 20 Q1 FY2027 report. Fundamental case: consensus $78.6-80.1B revenue +78-80% YoY, EPS ~$1.77 (Goldman $1.86, 7-8% above consensus); AMD's $10.25B Q1 +38% validates DC demand floor; hyperscaler $725B capex 2026 (+77%). Summit catalyst: H200 chip licensing framework under active discussion in Beijing — could restore $3.5-4B annual China revenue. BofA PT $320, consensus PT $272, 37/37 Strong Buy. Risk: NVDA historically falls post-earnings even on beats; QCOM −11% precedent; beat-and-raise may already be priced at $225.
flagged_setups.price_note: $225.83 NVDA close May 13 2026 — Yahoo Finance / 247wallst; pre-market May 14 ~$226.39

tase_events.event: TA-125 May 13 close: ~4,438 pts (−0.22%). Summit optimism + PPI shock create mixed signal. Iran ceasefire fragility remains direct regional security risk. TASE.TA Q1 2026 results were released May 12 after close (conference call May 12 8pm Israel time). TA-125 up +65.36% YoY — extraordinary run relative to global peers.

narrative_threads: Warsh succession lock-in: Fed Chair confirmed 54-45 on May 13; Powell exits May 16; Warsh inherits the worst inflation setup of any incoming Chair since Volcker — CPI 3.8%, PPI 6.0%, PCE 4.5%, Brent $108, TNX 4.49%, rate hike probability 30-35%; first FOMC June 16-17 will set the tone for his independence vs Trump alignment

Beijing summit chip-for-trade architecture: H200 licensing framework is the centrepiece technology deal; $3.5-4B China revenue restoration would be NVDA's largest single-summit catalyst; Huang personally invited by Trump signals chip policy is not an afterthought — it's the primary strategic agenda

NVDA May 20 compound setup: dual-catalyst (H200 deal + fundamental beat) makes this the most loaded NVDA earnings since the original AI breakout in May 2023; Goldman above-consensus at $1.86; 37/37 Strong Buy; but SOXX -5% May 12 precedent and QCOM -11% show valuation is not bulletproof

PPI pass-through accelerating: gasoline +15.6% MoM and trade services +2.7% confirm Iran war and tariff costs are now flowing into producer margins — CPI May (released ~June 12) will reveal if this feeds consumers; Warsh's first inflation reading before his first FOMC

Brent short thesis failing: Two consecutive short setups on Iran deal timing (no-007 −3.36%, no-005 −5.92%) confirm the deal remains perpetually 'end-of-month'; IEA warns undersupply until October even if conflict ends; Brent has stabilized in $105-110 range — the Citi $85-90 thesis requires a deal that keeps not arriving
